newsBriefing: Today's headline cycle crossed college hoops, baseball, MMA, soccer, golf and NFL roster news. Georgia Tech moved on from coach Damon Stoudamire after an ACC last-place finish, while UConn coach Dan Hurley was ejected in the final second of a loss to Marquette and later drew a $25,000 Big East fine. In the World Baseball Classic, Japan rallied late to beat Australia behind Masataka Yoshida's key homer, and Tigers ace Tarik Skubal said he is reconsidering his long-term U.S. WBC plans despite how hard it is to walk away. In football, the Packers re-signed offensive lineman Sean Rhyan to a three-year, $33 million deal, and in global star business Inter Miami owner Jorge Mas said Lionel Messi's total annual compensation lands in the $70 million to $80 million range. On the golf side, Jon Rahm captured LIV Hong Kong for his first individual title since 2024, while UFC president Dana White said Jon Jones and Conor McGregor are not expected on the proposed White House fight card.
